description: Create UsageCheckScheduler class, bootstrap module, API status endpoint, and update the Quota page UI to display worker status (next run, running state, last run info)
steps: Step 1: Create src/lib/usageCheckScheduler.ts - Implement UsageCheckScheduler class following the exact pattern from src/lib/providerModels/scheduler.ts. Key behaviors: (a) Use global.__appSingleton.usageCheckScheduler for singleton persistence across hot reload. (b) Fields: timerId, running, startedAt, nextRunAt, lastRun (with startedAt, status, message, refreshedCount, errorCount, totalConnections), settings (enabled, intervalMinutes - default 5 minutes). (c) loadSettings() reads from DB settings (key: usageCheckWorker). (d) start() method loads settings, schedules first run. (e) scheduleNext() uses setTimeout with .unref(), sets nextRunAt. (f) runScheduled() fetches all active OAuth connections from USAGE_SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS (use getCurrentProviderConnections from src/lib/connectionAccess.ts filtered by authType:'oauth' and isActive:true, then filter by USAGE_SUPPORTED_PROVIDERS), iterates over them calling runDedupedUsageRefreshJob with refreshUsageWithTransientSkip for each connection, tracks success/error counts. (g) stop() clears timer. (h) getStatus() returns { enabled, intervalMinutes, startedAt, nextRunAt, running, lastRun }. (i) Export getUsageCheckScheduler() singleton getter.

Step 2: Create src/lib/usageCheckScheduler/bootstrap.ts - Follow the pattern from src/lib/providerModels/bootstrap.ts. Export ensureUsageCheckSchedulerStarted() with a bootstrapped flag that calls getUsageCheckScheduler().start() once.

Step 3: Create src/app/api/usage-worker/status/route.ts - Implement GET handler that: (a) calls requireManagementAuth for auth, (b) calls ensureUsageCheckSchedulerStarted(), (c) returns getUsageCheckScheduler().getStatus() as JSON. Also implement POST handler to trigger a manual run (calls runScheduled on the scheduler).

Step 4: Wire the scheduler into existing usage API access - In src/app/api/usage/[connectionId]/route.ts, add a fire-and-forget call to ensureUsageCheckSchedulerStarted().catch(() => {}) at the start of the GET handler (following the pattern from src/app/api/v1/models/route.ts line 425).

Step 5: Update src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/usage/components/ProviderLimits/index.tsx - Add a worker status panel at the top of the component (after the CardHeader, before the filters). This panel should: (a) Fetch /api/usage-worker/status on mount and poll every 30 seconds while visible. (b) Display: running state (with spinner if active), next run time (formatted relative like 'in 3 minutes'), last run time and result (success count, error count), a 'Run Now' button that POSTs to /api/usage-worker/status. (c) Use shadcn/ui components (Badge, Button, Card) and Tailwind utilities for styling. (d) Show compact inline status - not a large separate card.
acceptance_criteria: UsageCheckScheduler class exists with start/stop/getStatus/runScheduled methods and uses global.__appSingleton pattern
GET /api/usage-worker/status returns scheduler status JSON with fields: enabled, intervalMinutes, startedAt, nextRunAt, running, lastRun
POST /api/usage-worker/status triggers a manual run of the scheduler and returns result
The scheduler is lazily bootstrapped when usage API endpoints are hit
The Quota page UI shows worker status: running indicator, next run time, last run result, and a manual trigger button
npm run typecheck passes with no new errors
verification: Run npx tsc --noEmit to confirm no TypeScript errors
Run npm run build to confirm the build succeeds with the new files
findings: TypeScript check passes clean. Build has a pre-existing failure on /dashboard/cli-tools page (TypeError: z?.toLowerCase is not a function in cliTools_ts) unrelated to our changes - verified by stashing changes and confirming same error on base. Tests: 1 pre-existing failure in status-handling-transient-auth.test.ts (syntax error in require call with TypeScript) unrelated to our changes. All 186 other test files pass.
